summary: Significant fuel allocation shortfall at Chicago O'Hare hub affecting Pacific Northwest and West Coast routes. Operations team implementing rolling schedule cuts to preserve long-haul fuel reserves.
operational_notes: Fuel uplift restricted to 90% of planned load. Ferry flights suspended until further notice. Gate teams briefed on rebooking procedures.

timeline.note: Initial fuel supply alert raised at ORD hub.
Pacific Northwest routes flagged for schedule review.
Cancellations initiated for high-load-factor routes.
Ongoing — 34 flights canceled, 8 aircraft grounded.

summary: Transatlantic routes subject to a fuel surcharge of $45–$80 per segment amid elevated kerosene spot prices in European markets. No schedule disruptions at this time.
operational_notes: Surcharge applied from booking date 2025-03-25. Commercial team monitoring European Jet CIF NWE benchmark.

timeline.note: European spot fuel prices rise 12% week-on-week.
Fuel surcharge of $45–$80 per segment applied.

summary: Nordic intra-regional network disrupted due to kerosene delivery delays at Oslo Gardermoen and Copenhagen Kastrup. Eighteen short-haul services canceled across Scandinavian routes.
operational_notes: Contract renegotiation with Norwegian fuel supplier ongoing. Temporary reduction in turnaround fuel loads across OSL/CPH.

timeline.note: Fuel delivery delays reported at OSL by ground operations.
Emergency fuel contract activated — capacity constrained.
18 flights canceled across the Nordic hub network.
Negotiations ongoing; partial delivery resumed at CPH.

summary: Long-haul services to Bangkok and Beijing suspended temporarily due to uneconomic fuel cost structure. Routes under commercial review for Q2 continuation.
operational_notes: Aircraft redeployed to higher-margin intra-European routes during suspension period.

timeline.note: Route profitability review triggered by fuel cost spike.
BKK and PEK departures suspended effective April 1.

summary: American's primary hub at Dallas/Fort Worth has experienced the most significant fuel supply disruption in its network. Forty-seven mainline departures canceled across major domestic corridors.
operational_notes: Primary fuel supplier invoked force majeure clause. Regional carrier partners also affected. Secondary supplier onboarding underway.

timeline.note: DFW primary fuel supplier declares supply force majeure event.
AA activates contingency operations plan.
First wave of 22 cancellations processed for March 30.
25 additional cancellations added for March 31–April 2.
Ongoing — secondary supplier being onboarded at DFW.

summary: Caribbean leisure routes flagged for elevated fuel-risk status. Limited cancellations to date; situation is being monitored closely as regional supply chain remains under pressure.
operational_notes: MIA fuel reserves adequate for approximately 72 hours at current uplift rates. Caribbean ops team on heightened alert.

timeline.note: Caribbean routes added to fuel risk watch list.
9 flights canceled; remainder still operating normally.

summary: Air New Zealand has cut transpacific and Southeast Asian long-haul services following unsustainable jet-fuel procurement costs at Auckland Airport. Twelve departures removed from the April schedule.
operational_notes: Aircraft redeployed to domestic NZ network while long-haul fuel supply is normalized. Passengers rebooking on partner carriers.

timeline.note: AKL fuel procurement costs exceed budgeted threshold by 18%.
Transpacific schedule reviewed; LAX/SFO frequency cut.
HKG and SIN routes added to service reduction scope.
12 departures removed from April long-haul schedule.

summary: Domestic New Zealand network fares increased by NZD 20–55 per segment due to elevated fuel costs. Limited cancellations; schedule maintained with reduced aircraft utilization.
operational_notes: ATR turboprop fleet temporarily substituted on shorter sectors in place of Boeing 737.

timeline.note: Domestic fare surcharge of NZD 20–55 per segment applied.
7 domestic flights canceled; primary schedule maintained.
